TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Empire of Men Faction.

Translations.Comment: Государства (states/realms) emphasizes multiple kingdoms united. Plural form maintains grand scale typical of fantasy empires.
Имперский (imperial) + Мечник (swordsman). Standard infantry term consistent with military hierarchy.
Пешие (foot/on foot) distinguishes from mounted cavalry. Maintains distinction between heavy infantry and mounted knights.
Алебардист (halberdier) is professional Russian military term for elite polearm troops in imperial service.
Копейщик (spearman) is traditional Russian term. Standard formation troops forming backbone of army.
Рыцари (knights) denotes mounted elite cavalry. Direct term reflecting their status as empire's elite warriors.
Лёгкая Кавалерия (light cavalry) for scouts and skirmishers. Contrasts with heavy mounted knights.
Лучники (archers) is classic ranged infantry. Consistent fantasy wargame terminology.
Арбалетчики (crossbowmen) represents more advanced ranged troops than basic archers. Professional military term.
Аркебузиры (arquebusiers) is historical term used in Russian military for Renaissance-era firearms troops.
Пушка (cannon) is direct term for heavy artillery. Singular emphasizes individual cannon placement.
Миномёт (mortar) for indirect fire artillery. Modern Russian term applied to fantasy wargame context.
Колосс (colossus) conveys massive war machine. Maintains fantasy tone better than transliterated Dreadnought.
Гриф (gryphon) is standard Russian term. Majestic symbol of imperial might and power in fantasy tradition.
Дракон (dragon) under imperial control. Ultimate flying unit; majestic and powerful term.
Капитан (captain) is standard Russian military rank. Battle-hardened commander of troops.
Командант (commandant) + Рыцарей (of knights). Leader of elite knightly orders.
Волшебник (wizard) in imperial service. Classic fantasy term for magic user with mystical authority.
